data.title: Jan3 CEO: 2025 will be a bear market, but Bitcoin will enter a long-term bull market that lasts until 2035.
data.description: Jan3 CEO Samson Mow stated on the X platform that 2025 will be a bear market, but Bitcoin may be about to enter a bull market that lasts until 2035.

data.title: LayerZero's third vote on the "fee switch" failed to reach the quorum requirement and will remain closed.
data.description: The LayerZero Foundation officially announced on X that the third vote on opening the "fee switch" has concluded. Due to insufficient quorum, LayerZero's "fee switch" will remain closed. The next vote will be held in six months.

data.title: An address suspected of being linked to the YGG vault deposited 40 million YGG tokens, worth $2.65 million, into Binance.
data.description: Arkham monitoring shows that about seven hours ago, an address starting with "0xE040" deposited 40 million YGG tokens into Binance, worth approximately $2.65 million. This address is not from an ordinary trader. On-chain analysis suggests that the wallet is likely directly linked to the vault of the blockchain game guild Yield Guild Games (YGG), but it is not yet confirmed whether the YGG team's vault will be sold off.

data.title: Bitcoin Market Trends Show Whales Buying During Price Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, CryptoQuant founder Ki Young Ju has analyzed the distribution of Bitcoin spot order volumes. The data indicates a pattern during Bitcoin's recent price fluctuations: retail investors tend to buy when prices rise, while whales sell; conversely, when prices fall, retail investors sell and whales buy. The market is currently in a phase where prices are declining, and whales are purchasing.

data.title: Bitcoin saw bear market in 2025, 'decade long' bull run ahead: Mow
data.description:
Bitcoin reached new all-time highs in October, yet Jan3 founder Samson Mow has described the year as a “bear market” and anticipates a major bull run ahead.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-bear-market-bull-decade-ahead-jan3-samson-mow?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Coinbase CEO: The Genius Act will not be amended; this is Coinbase's red line.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated in a post on the X platform that banks are lobbying Congress to amend the Genius Act. Coinbase will not allow any amendments to this act; it is Coinbase's red line, and they will continue to protect the interests of their customers and the cryptocurrency industry. Armstrong added that he personally predicts that in a few years, once banks realize the enormous business opportunities presented by stablecoins, they will change their stance and lobby Congress to allow stablecoins to pay interest and yield. Therefore, their current efforts are completely futile (and unethical), and the innovator's dilemma remains. Currently, the Genius Act prohibits stablecoin issuers from offering stablecoin rewards, but trading platforms like Gemini, Coinbase, and Kraken can, and this is already included in the existing legislation. If traditional bankers amend the act to prohibit such behavior, it will stifle stablecoin innovation.

data.title: JPMorgan Freezes Accounts of Stablecoin Startups Over Risks
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase freezes accounts of stablecoin startups linked to Venezuela due to high-risk factors.
source: https://coincu.com/news/jpmorgan-freezes-stablecoin-accounts/

data.title: Trump Calls for Release of Names in Epstein Case
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump announced on the Truth platform that millions of new pages of information have been discovered in the Epstein case. He stated that the Department of Justice is being forced to dedicate all its time to addressing what he described as a Democrat-driven hoax. Trump instructed the Department of Justice to release the names of all individuals involved.

data.title: Largest U.S. State Pension Fund Expands Exposure to Bitcoin Treasury Firm Strategy
data.description:
The California Public Employees’ Retirement System (CalPERS), the largest U. S. state pension fund, has increased its exposure to Strategy ($MSTR), a company known for holding Bitcoin as a treasury asset. According to the latest disclosure, CalPERS has purchased 183,444 shares, with the position valued at approximately $29 million so far this year
source: https://www.ethnews.com/largest-u-s-state-pension-fund-expands-exposure-to-bitcoin-treasury-firm-strategy/

data.title: JPMorgan Chase freezes multiple stablecoin startup accounts, involving high-risk markets such as Venezuela.
data.description: In recent months, JPMorgan Chase has frozen accounts belonging to at least two rapidly growing stablecoin startups operating in high-risk countries such as Venezuela. The bank's move highlights the risks associated with cryptocurrency transactions, as banks must understand their business dealings and the source of funds. The bank also froze Blindpay's accounts. (The Information)

data.title: JPMorgan Freezes Accounts of Stablecoin Startups in High-Risk Regions
data.description: According to Odaily, JPMorgan has recently frozen accounts of at least two rapidly growing stablecoin startups operating in high-risk countries like Venezuela. This move highlights the risks banks face with cryptocurrency transactions, as they must ensure they understand their business partners and the origins of funds. The bank specifically froze the account of Blindpay.

data.title: Trust Wallet Exploit Drains $7M: Hundreds Of Users Affected
data.description:
Amid already fragile sentiment across the crypto market, attackers exploited Trust Wallet, shaking confidence in self-custody solutions. The breach has impacted hundreds of users, with on-chain data showing that more than $6. 77 million has already been stolen. The timing has amplified concern, coming at a moment when investors are already navigating heightened uncertainty, declining prices, and rising risk aversion
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/trust-wallet-exploit-drains-7m-hundreds-of-users-affected/

data.title: Lighter: The final batch of points for Season 2 has been distributed. Witch Points have been removed and redistributed.
data.description: A Lighter official stated on the Discord community: "Today we have distributed the final batch of points for Season 2. At the same time, we have also removed and redistributed points from sybil, self-trading, and wash-trading."

data.title: CryptoQuant Analyst: Total ETH Contract Trading Volume Hits New Record This Year
data.description: CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost wrote that Ethereum futures trading volume this year far exceeds previous years. Taking Binance as an example, Ethereum futures trading volume exceeded $6.74 trillion over the past year, almost double that of 2024, which already set a record. Therefore, Ethereum can be considered one of the most traded assets in the global derivatives market in 2025, highlighting the strength of speculative demand. Over the past year, the spot-to-futures ratio was approximately 0.2, meaning that for every $1 invested in ETH on the Binance spot market, nearly $5 was invested in futures contracts. This ratio is characteristic of a highly leveraged market, reflecting extreme speculation on Ethereum in 2025. Record trading volume coupled with such an unbalanced ratio indicates that futures largely determine Ethereum's price movements. Consequently, Ethereum's price volatility this year has often been amplified, disorderly, and highly dependent on liquidations, ultimately resulting in it reaching an all-time high by a mere few dollars.

data.title: Investors pull $1 trillion from active equity funds after weak stock selection
data.description:
Investors spent 2025 pulling money, not adding it. Roughly $1 trillion left active equity mutual funds as frustration built around weak stock selection and a market that refused to reward diversity. The S&P 500 kept hitting records, but the gains came from the same tight group of seven American tech giants. Owning anything else meant […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/investors-pull-1t-from-active-equity-funds/

data.title: Zcash privacy adoption holds firm as shielded supply stabilizes at 23%
data.description:
Zcash’s private supply has stayed steady at 23% after rising from 8% earlier in 2025.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/zcash-privacy-adoption-holds-firm/

data.title: Michael Burry Criticizes Bitcoin Valuation in Podcast Discussion
data.description: According to Foresight News, Michael Burry, the hedge fund manager who inspired the film 'The Big Short,' expressed strong skepticism about Bitcoin during a recent appearance on the podcast 'Against the Rules.' Burry compared Bitcoin to the tulip mania of the past, suggesting that it holds no intrinsic value. He criticized the notion of Bitcoin reaching $100,000, calling it absurd. Burry remarked on the casual manner in which some commentators discuss Bitcoin's price fluctuations, noting that they often mention its rise to $100,000 and subsequent drop to $98,000 without much concern.

data.title: Ex‑Coinbase support agent in India arrested in probe of crypto exchange hack
data.description:
A former Coinbase support agent in India has been arrested after being linked to a massive security breach that shook the crypto giant earlier this year. The arrest, confirmed by both Coinbase and Indian police in Hyderabad, came months after hackers bribed customer service staff to steal customer information. This triggered a $20 million ransom […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ex%e2%80%91coinbase-support-agent-in-india-arrested/
